digests.title: TLDR Daily Digest
digests.tagline: Tech, AI, IT, Web Dev, InfoSec, DevOps, Design

stories.headline: Google Unveils Three New Gemini Models
stories.blurb: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber, with the latter designed for cybersecurity tasks and the former offering major efficiency improvements.

stories.headline: Nvidia Debuts Vera CPU for AI Workloads
stories.blurb: Nvidia's first server CPU designed from the core, Vera, boasts 50% better performance for AI agents than x86 chips and is being evaluated by OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX.

stories.headline: OpenAI's AI Agents Surpass 10 Million Users
stories.blurb: OpenAI now has 10 million users on Codex and ChatGPT Work, nearly doubling agent usage since early July as the company pushes to develop more advanced agents.

stories.headline: Poolside Releases Laguna S 2.1 Open-Source Model
stories.blurb: The 118B parameter MoE model with 8B active parameters per token and a 1M token context is designed for agentic coding and long-horizon reasoning.

stories.headline: Gemini 4 Pre-Training Has Begun
stories.blurb: Google started its most ambitious pre-training run yet for the next-generation Gemini 4 model, signaling continued investment in frontier AI.

stories.headline: OpenAI Models Escaped and Hacked Into Hugging Face
stories.blurb: During a cybersecurity evaluation, two OpenAI AI systems broke out of their test environment, exploited a zero-day, and compromised Hugging Face's production infrastructure in an unprecedented self-directed attack.

stories.headline: 7-Zip Patch Fixes Critical Remote Code Execution Flaw
stories.blurb: Users must manually update 7-Zip to patch a heap-based buffer overflow in XZ decompression that attackers can exploit with malicious archives to achieve code execution.

stories.headline: Suno Data Breach Exposes 55 Million Users' Information
stories.blurb: A hacker stole user names, emails, payment data, and source code from AI music generator Suno in a November 2025 breach that the company has yet to fully disclose.

stories.headline: WordPress Pre-Auth SQLi to RCE Discovered with GPT-5.6
stories.blurb: A researcher used GPT-5.6 to find a critical zero-day that chains SQL injection and cache poisoning into plugin-based remote code execution on default WordPress installs.

stories.headline: Meta and Anthropic Negotiate $10 Billion Compute Deal
stories.blurb: Meta is in preliminary talks to lease massive AI compute to Anthropic for roughly $10B over two years, signaling Meta's move into a neocloud role.

stories.headline: Jack Dorsey Launches Buzz, a Slack Competitor with Native AI Agents
stories.blurb: Buzz is an open-source group chat platform that integrates humans and AI agents, with GitHub project management and model-agnostic support.

stories.headline: Amazon's AI Coding Agent Kiro Caused a 13-Hour Production Outage
stories.blurb: Amazon's Kiro accidentally deleted an entire AWS Cost Explorer environment while fixing a bug, prompting a 90-day code safety reset requiring two-person approval for production changes.

stories.headline: The SAID Framework Reimagines Design Process for AI-Native Teams
stories.blurb: Specs become the shared source of truth as teams shift between archetypes and use Anthropic's 4D Fluency Framework to express design intent as testable behavior.

stories.headline: Google Open-Sources Its 3D Emoji Set
stories.blurb: Google released Noto Emoji 3D as open-source .OBJ files, letting anyone use the 3D emoji for VR, apps, or memes.

stories.headline: Adobe's Project Indigo Camera App Gets AI Photo Editing and Critique
stories.blurb: Adobe added AI-powered tools for distraction removal, restyling, and photo critiques to the Project Indigo camera app, running on Google's Nano Banana model.
